title: centaurea sterilis subsp. vankovii
intro: Centaurea sterilis subsp. vankovii, also known as Balkan knapweed or Vankov's centaury, is a subspecies of the Centaurea sterilis plant, which belongs to the daisy family and is native to the Balkan Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Centaurea sterilis subsp. vankovii include reducing inflammation, fighting against microbial infections, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
